question: What should you avoid doing when you have a pimple?
options: Keeping it clean and moisturised
Popping or squeezing it
Applying a spot treatment
Leaving it alone to heal

question: What is a whitehead?
options: A pimple filled with blood
A clogged pore that is closed at the surface and appears as a small white bump
A large painful cyst under the skin
A dark spot left after a pimple heals

question: What is a blackhead?
options: A dark scar left by acne
A clogged pore that is open at the surface and oxidises turning dark
A type of mole on the skin
A bruise caused by squeezing pimples

question: Why do blackheads appear dark or black?
options: Because dirt is trapped inside them
Because the sebum oxidises when exposed to air turning it dark
Because they contain dried blood
Because they are infected pores

question: What causes acne to form?
options: Only eating junk food
A combination of excess oil clogged pores bacteria and inflammation
Only stress and anxiety
Only not washing your face enough

question: What is cystic acne?
options: Small surface whiteheads
Large painful pimples that form deep under the skin and can cause scarring
A type of acne only teenagers get
Tiny red dots caused by sun exposure

question: Which bacteria is mainly responsible for causing acne breakouts?
options: E. coli
Cutibacterium acnes previously called P. acnes
Staphylococcus
Streptococcus

question: What does benzoyl peroxide do to treat acne?
options: Hydrates and soothes inflamed pimples
Kills acne-causing bacteria and helps clear pores
Brightens acne scars overnight
Permanently removes blackheads

question: What does salicylic acid do for acne-prone skin?
options: Kills acne bacteria on the surface
Exfoliates inside pores to prevent clogging and treat blackheads
Provides hydration to dry acne skin
Reduces redness caused by cystic acne

question: What is a spot treatment?
options: A full-face acne cream applied everywhere
A targeted product applied directly onto individual pimples to help them heal faster
A treatment that removes all acne scars
A sunscreen for acne-prone skin

question: Can stress cause or worsen acne breakouts?
options: No stress has no effect on skin
Yes stress triggers hormones that increase oil production and can worsen acne
Stress only causes acne in teenagers
Stress only causes dark spots not pimples

question: Why should you never pop a pimple?
options: Because it makes the pimple last longer which wastes time
Because it can push bacteria deeper cause infection and lead to scarring
Because popping pimples spreads acne to others
Because popping pimples causes permanent skin damage

question: What is the term for dark marks left on skin after a pimple heals?
options: Active acne
Cystic acne
Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation or PIH
Rosacea

question: What ingredient helps fade dark marks left behind by acne?
options: Benzoyl peroxide
Salicylic acid
Niacinamide or vitamin C
Glycerin

question: Can adults get acne even in their 30s and 40s?
options: No acne stops completely after teenage years
Yes adult acne is very common especially hormonal acne
Only women over 30 get adult acne
Adult acne is always more severe than teenage acne

question: What is hormonal acne and where does it typically appear?
options: Acne caused by eating too much sugar appearing on the forehead
Acne triggered by hormonal fluctuations often appearing along the jawline and chin
Acne caused by stress appearing on the cheeks
Acne caused by not washing your face appearing on the nose

question: Which of these habits can make acne worse?
options: Changing your pillowcase regularly
Using non-comedogenic products
Touching your face frequently throughout the day
Drinking plenty of water

question: How does touching your face contribute to breakouts?
options: It has no effect on acne
It transfers dirt oil and bacteria from your hands to your pores which can trigger breakouts
Only touching with dirty tools causes breakouts
Only touching your nose causes breakouts

question: What does non-comedogenic mean on a skincare or makeup product?
options: It is fragrance free
It will not clog pores which is important for acne-prone skin
It contains acne-fighting ingredients
It is suitable for sensitive skin only

question: What is a comedone?
options: A type of acne scar
A clogged hair follicle that is the basic form of acne including blackheads and whiteheads
A painful cyst deep under the skin
A red inflamed nodule

question: Can dirty pillowcases contribute to acne breakouts?
options: No pillowcases have no effect on skin
Yes dirty pillowcases harbour bacteria and oil that can transfer to your skin while you sleep
Only silk pillowcases cause breakouts
Only people with oily skin are affected by pillowcases

question: What is skin purging and how is it different from a breakout caused by a bad product?
options: They are exactly the same thing and both mean stop using the product
Purging is a temporary increase in breakouts as skin adjusts to active ingredients while a bad reaction is ongoing and worsens
Purging only happens with vitamin C products
Purging lasts for at least a year before skin clears

question: What type of acne is most likely to leave permanent scars?
options: Small surface whiteheads
Tiny blackheads
Deep cystic or nodular acne
Mild hormonal pimples on the chin

question: What does it mean when your acne is described as inflammatory?
options: It only appears in summer when it is hot
It involves red swollen pimples caused by the body's immune response to bacteria
It is caused only by inflammatory foods
It is a type of acne that only appears on the back

question: What is the difference between a papule and a pustule?
options: They are exactly the same type of pimple
A papule is a red raised bump with no pus and a pustule is a pimple with visible white or yellow pus
A papule is bigger than a pustule
A pustule is found on the body and a papule is only on the face

question: Can hair products cause forehead and hairline breakouts?
options: No hair products never affect facial skin
Yes oily or heavy hair products can transfer to the forehead and clog pores causing breakouts
Only hair dye causes forehead acne
Only dry shampoo causes hairline pimples

question: What is the term for acne caused by wearing helmets masks or tight clothing that traps sweat?
options: Hormonal acne
Cystic acne
Acne mechanica caused by friction and pressure on skin
Nodular acne

question: Can wearing a face mask cause acne and what is this nicknamed?
options: No masks protect skin from pollution
Yes it is nicknamed maskne and is caused by friction sweat and heat under the mask
Only surgical masks cause maskne
Maskne only affects people with existing acne

question: What food group is most commonly associated with worsening acne according to research?
options: Fruits and vegetables
High glycaemic index foods and dairy products in some people
Healthy fats like avocado
Protein-rich foods like eggs

question: Does drinking water help with acne?
options: Yes water alone will cure all acne
Staying hydrated supports overall skin health but water alone is not a cure for acne
Water makes acne worse by hydrating bacteria
Only cold water helps with acne

question: What is the safest way to deal with a blind pimple that has not come to a head?
options: Squeeze it as hard as possible
Apply a warm compress to encourage it to surface and use a salicylic acid spot treatment
Pick at it with a needle at home
Ignore it and never apply anything to it

question: Can sunscreen cause acne breakouts?
options: All sunscreens always cause breakouts
Some heavy sunscreens can clog pores but non-comedogenic formulas are designed for acne-prone skin
Sunscreen always clears acne by blocking UV rays
Only mineral sunscreen causes breakouts

question: What is acne on the back commonly nicknamed?
options: Back bumps
Backne
Spine acne
Dorsal acne

question: What should you look for in a foundation if you have acne-prone skin?
options: The heaviest full coverage formula available
A non-comedogenic and oil-free formula that won't clog pores
Any foundation with shimmer and glow finish
A foundation with fragrance for freshness

question: Why does acne often get worse during a woman's menstrual cycle?
options: Because women wash their face less often during that time
Hormonal fluctuations increase oil production which can trigger breakouts
Because makeup is worn more heavily during that time
Menstrual cycles have no effect on acne

question: What is the role of a dermatologist when it comes to severe acne?
options: Dermatologists only treat skin cancer not acne
A dermatologist can prescribe stronger treatments like antibiotics retinoids or hormonal therapy for severe acne
Dermatologists only recommend over the counter products
Dermatologists only treat acne scars not active acne

question: What is tretinoin and why is it used for acne?
options: A gentle over the counter moisturiser
A prescription-strength retinoid that speeds up skin cell turnover to unclog pores and treat acne
A type of antibiotic pill for severe acne
A natural plant extract for mild breakouts

question: Can over-exfoliating make acne worse?
options: No exfoliating as much as possible always helps acne
Yes over-exfoliating can damage the skin barrier cause irritation and worsen breakouts
Exfoliating has no effect on acne
Only physical scrubs worsen acne not chemical exfoliants

question: What is the right way to wash your face when you have acne?
options: Scrub hard to remove all oil and bacteria
Use a gentle acne-friendly cleanser twice a day with lukewarm water and soft motions
Wash your face five or more times a day
Use very hot water to open pores and kill bacteria

question: Does exercising help or hurt acne?
options: Exercise always directly causes more breakouts
Exercise can benefit skin by reducing stress but sweat left on skin can clog pores so cleansing after workouts is important
Exercise has zero effect on acne
Only outdoor exercise causes breakouts

question: What common kitchen ingredient do people often incorrectly use as an acne treatment?
options: Coconut oil
Toothpaste
Honey
Olive oil

question: Why is toothpaste not a good spot treatment for pimples?
options: Toothpaste is too expensive to use on skin
Toothpaste can cause irritation burning and make the pimple worse because it is not formulated for skin
Toothpaste only works on blackheads not whiteheads
Toothpaste is too gentle to have any effect on pimples

question: What percentage of people experience acne at some point in their lives?
options: About 10 percent of people
About 25 percent of people
Up to 85 percent of people experience acne at some point
Only 5 percent of people ever get acne

question: What is a comedone extractor tool and should you use one at home?
options: A tool that applies spot treatment precisely and is safe for home use
A metal tool used to extract blackheads and pimples that should ideally only be used by trained professionals
A facial roller that prevents comedones from forming
A cleansing brush that prevents blackheads

question: Can changing your diet help improve acne?
options: No diet has no proven connection to acne
For some people reducing high sugar dairy and processed foods can help reduce breakouts
Only eating more vegetables completely cures acne
Diet only affects body acne not facial acne

question: What does it mean when acne is described as non-inflammatory?
options: It means acne that does not respond to treatment
It refers to blackheads and whiteheads which are clogged pores without redness or swelling
It means acne that appears only on the body
It refers to acne that disappears without any treatment

question: Which vitamin is sometimes used in high doses to treat severe acne under medical supervision?
options: Vitamin C
Vitamin D
Vitamin E
Vitamin A in the form of isotretinoin also known as Accutane

question: What is a good affordable everyday habit to prevent acne from worsening?
options: Applying heavy makeup to cover all pimples
Washing your hands regularly and avoiding touching your face throughout the day
Only washing your face once a week
Using the strongest acid exfoliants every day

question: Which of these is a sign that a pimple is becoming infected and may need medical attention?
options: A small whitehead appearing on the chin
A pimple that stays the same size for a few days
A pimple that becomes increasingly large painful hot and filled with pus
A tiny blackhead on the nose

question: What is the best attitude to have toward acne to protect your mental wellbeing?
options: Feel ashamed and cover it with layers of heavy makeup always
Remember that acne is extremely common affects most people at some point and does not define your worth or beauty
Avoid all social situations until acne completely clears
Only talk to a dermatologist and never discuss it with anyone else
